title: sebastiania glandulosa var. sellowiana
intro: Sebastiania glandulosa var. sellowiana is a plant species, also known as Brazilian copaiba or copaiba tree, native to the tropical regions of South America. It is renowned for its medicinal properties, particularly its resin, which contains a high concentration of bicyclic sesquiterpene, an anti-inflammatory compound that has been used to treat various health conditions. The resin of the plant has been used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits in treating infections and promoting wound healing. The resin can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including essential oils, extracts, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the specific condition being treated.
